sentences.base_language: The eve of the speech
sentences.target_language: 演讲前夕
sentences.romanized: yǎnjiǎng qiányè
sentences.grammarNote: **演讲** (yǎnjiǎng) = speech/lecture: **演** (perform/demonstrate) + **讲** (speak/lecture). **前夕** (qiányè) = eve/the night before: **前** (before) + **夕** (evening/eve). **前夕** is literary — compare the everyday **前一天晚上** (the night before). This is the story's title, establishing its literary register.

sentences.base_language: I had been preparing for this speech for a whole month
sentences.target_language: 我已经为这次演讲准备了整整一个月了
sentences.romanized: wǒ yǐjīng wèi zhè cì yǎnjiǎng zhǔnbèi le zhěngzhěng yī gè yuè le
sentences.grammarNote:
**为** (wèi) = for / on behalf of — marks the purpose or beneficiary: 为这次演讲准备 = 'prepare for this speech.'
**整整** (zhěngzhěng) = a full / a whole — intensifies duration: 整整一个月 = a full month, every bit of it. The double **了** pattern (verb + 了 ... 了) indicates an ongoing situation that has reached the stated duration.

sentences.base_language: Even so, I still felt extremely nervous
sentences.target_language: 即使这样，我还是感到非常紧张
sentences.romanized: jíshǐ zhèyàng, wǒ háishi gǎndào fēicháng jǐnzhāng
sentences.grammarNote:
**即使** (jíshǐ) = even if / even so — a stronger concession than **虽然** (although). **即使…还是/也…** = even if… still…
**紧张** (jǐnzhāng) = nervous/tense: **紧** (tight) + **张** (stretched). Used for nerves before an event, also for 'tense' situations.

sentences.base_language: I put all my notes on the desk and read them over again
sentences.target_language: 我把所有的笔记都放在桌子上，重新看了一遍
sentences.romanized: wǒ bǎ suǒyǒu de bǐjì dōu fàng zài zhuōzi shàng, chóngxīn kàn le yī biàn
sentences.grammarNote:
**把** construction: 把 + object (笔记) + verb + result/location (放在桌子上) — emphasizes what is done to the object.
**一遍** (yī biàn) = once through / one time (for an action done start to finish). Compare **一次** (one occurrence) — **遍** specifically means doing something from beginning to end.

sentences.base_language: Although I had already memorized the key points, I was still very worried
sentences.target_language: 虽然我已经把要点背下来了，但我还是很担心
sentences.romanized: suīrán wǒ yǐjīng bǎ yàodiǎn bèi xiàlái le, dàn wǒ háishi hěn dānxīn
sentences.grammarNote:
**虽然…但是/但…** = although… still… — the classic concessive pair. **还是** (háishi) = still (despite everything), reinforcing the contrast.
**背下来** = memorize (commit to memory): **背** (recite from memory) + **下来** (directional complement meaning to bring/fix something down into place).

sentences.base_language: My roommate noticed that I seemed anxious and asked me what was wrong
sentences.target_language: 我室友发现我看起来心烦意乱，问我怎么了
sentences.romanized: wǒ shìyǒu fāxiàn wǒ kàn qǐlái xīn fán yì luàn, wèn wǒ zěnme le
sentences.grammarNote:
**心烦意乱** (xīn fán yì luàn) is a four-character idiom (成语 chéngyǔ): heart-troubled-mind-chaotic → agitated, flustered. 成语 are a key feature of written and formal Chinese.
**怎么了** (zěnme le) = what's wrong / what happened? — a very common, natural way to check on someone.

sentences.base_language: I told him I was afraid I would forget what I wanted to say
sentences.target_language: 我告诉他我怕自己会忘记想说的话
sentences.romanized: wǒ gàosu tā wǒ pà zìjǐ huì wàngjì xiǎng shuō de huà
sentences.grammarNote:
**自己** (zìjǐ) = oneself / myself — used as a reflexive pronoun to refer back to the subject. 怕自己会… = 'afraid that I myself would…'
**想说的话** = 'the things I wanted to say': verb phrase + **的** + noun creates a relative clause. **话** (huà) = words/speech.

sentences.base_language: He said that preparation is the most important thing, and I had already done that
sentences.target_language: 他说准备是最重要的，而我已经做到了
sentences.romanized: tā shuō zhǔnbèi shì zuì zhòngyào de, ér wǒ yǐjīng zuò dào le
sentences.grammarNote:
**而** (ér) = and / whereas — a literary connector linking two clauses, often with mild contrast or addition. More formal than **然后** or **还**.
**做到** (zuò dào) = accomplish / manage to do: **做** (do) + **到** (reach/arrive at the goal). 做到了 = 'achieved it / got it done.'

sentences.base_language: His words made me feel a little better
sentences.target_language: 他的话让我感觉好了一些
sentences.romanized: tā de huà ràng wǒ gǎnjué hǎo le yīxiē
sentences.grammarNote:
**让** (ràng) = make / cause / let — causative verb: 他的话让我感觉好了 = 'His words made me feel better.'
**一些** (yīxiē) = a little / somewhat (placed after an adjective or verb): 好了一些 = a bit better. **了** marks the change in state (from less good to a bit better).

sentences.base_language: I practiced the speech in front of the mirror three more times
sentences.target_language: 我又在镜子前把演讲练习了三遍
sentences.romanized: wǒ yòu zài jìngzi qián bǎ yǎnjiǎng liànxí le sān biàn
sentences.grammarNote:
**又** (yòu) = again (for something already done or repeated in the past). Compare **再** (zài) = again in the future: 我再练一遍 (I'll practice once more).
**三遍** (sān biàn) = three times through — **遍** counts complete run-throughs. 练习了三遍 = 'practiced three times from start to finish.'

sentences.base_language: The more I practiced the more confident I felt
sentences.target_language: 我练得越多，感觉越自信
sentences.romanized: wǒ liàn de yuè duō, gǎnjué yuè zìxìn
sentences.grammarNote:
**越…越…** (yuè…yuè…) = the more… the more…: 越多越好 (the more the better), 越快越好 (the faster the better).
**自信** (zìxìn) = confident / self-confident: **自** (self) + **信** (trust/believe). 有自信 (have confidence), 充满自信 (full of confidence).

sentences.base_language: I finally managed to fall asleep at midnight
sentences.target_language: 我终于在午夜睡着了
sentences.romanized: wǒ zhōngyú zài wǔyè shuì zháo le
sentences.grammarNote:
**终于** (zhōngyú) = finally / at last — implies a long wait or effort before the result. Always precedes the verb.
**睡着** (shuì zháo) = fall asleep (successfully): **睡** (sleep) + **着** (resultative complement: achieve / catch). Compare: **睡不着** (shuì bù zháo) = can't fall asleep — a very common phrase for insomnia.

sentences.base_language: The next morning I woke up at seven
sentences.target_language: 第二天早上我七点就醒了
sentences.romanized: dì èr tiān zǎoshang wǒ qī diǎn jiù xǐng le
sentences.grammarNote:
**第** (dì) makes ordinal numbers: 第一 (first), 第二 (second), 第三 (third). 第二天 = 'the second day' = the next day.
**就** (jiù) before a verb conveys 'as early as' or 'already by that point': 七点就醒了 = 'woke up as early as seven' — implying it felt early, perhaps due to nerves.

sentences.base_language: I ate breakfast quickly and went over my key points one last time
sentences.target_language: 我快速吃完早饭，把重点最后过了一遍
sentences.romanized: wǒ kuàisù chī wán zǎofàn, bǎ zhòngdiǎn zuìhòu guò le yī biàn
sentences.grammarNote:
**吃完** = finish eating — resultative complement **完** (wán) indicates completion.
**过** (guò) here means 'go over / review' (not the experience marker): 过一遍 = 'go through it once.' **最后** (zuìhòu) = finally / last — marks the final action in a sequence. **重点** (zhòngdiǎn) = key points: **重** (important) + **点** (point).

sentences.base_language: When I walked into the hall it was already packed with people
sentences.target_language: 当我走进礼堂的时候，里面已经坐满了人
sentences.romanized: dāng wǒ zǒu jìn lǐtáng de shíhou, lǐmiàn yǐjīng zuò mǎn le rén
sentences.grammarNote:
**当…的时候** (dāng…de shíhou) = when… — sets the scene for a simultaneous or just-preceding event.
**坐满了人** = filled/packed with seated people: **满** (mǎn, full) is a resultative complement — 坐满 = 'sit until full.' **礼堂** (lǐtáng) = auditorium/hall: **礼** (ceremony) + **堂** (hall).

sentences.base_language: My heart started beating faster
sentences.target_language: 我的心跳开始加快
sentences.romanized: wǒ de xīntiào kāishǐ jiākuài
sentences.grammarNote:
**心跳** (xīntiào) = heartbeat: **心** (heart) + **跳** (jump/beat).
**开始** (kāishǐ) + verb = start to do something: 开始加快 = 'started to speed up.' **加快** (jiākuài) = accelerate / speed up: **加** (add/increase) + **快** (fast). The opposite is **减慢** (jiǎnmàn, slow down).

sentences.base_language: When they called my name I took a deep breath and walked up to the stage
sentences.target_language: 当他们叫到我的名字时，我深呼吸了一下，走上了舞台
sentences.romanized: dāng tāmen jiào dào wǒ de míngzi shí, wǒ shēn hūxī le yīxià, zǒu shàng le wǔtái
sentences.grammarNote:
**叫到** = 'called (until it reached)' — **到** as a resultative complement shows the action reached its target.
**一下** (yīxià) softens the action: 呼吸了一下 = 'took a breath' (brief, one-off). **深呼吸** (shēn hūxī) = deep breath: **深** (deep) + **呼吸** (breathe). **走上** = walked up onto — **上** shows upward directional movement.

sentences.base_language: The first thirty seconds felt like the hardest part
sentences.target_language: 前三十秒感觉是最难熬的部分
sentences.romanized: qián sānshí miǎo gǎnjué shì zuì nán áo de bùfen
sentences.grammarNote:
**难熬** (nán áo) = hard to endure / grueling: **难** (difficult) + **熬** (endure/suffer through). **熬** specifically evokes getting through something painful or prolonged: 熬夜 (stay up all night), 熬过去 (get through it).
**最难熬的部分** = the hardest part to endure — **最** + adj. = superlative.

sentences.base_language: But once I got through the opening the rest flowed out naturally
sentences.target_language: 但是一旦我过了开头，后面就自然而然地说出来了
sentences.romanized: dànshì yīdàn wǒ guò le kāitóu, hòumiàn jiù zìrán'érrán de shuō chūlái le
sentences.grammarNote:
**一旦…就…** (yīdàn…jiù…) = once… then… — indicates that as soon as a condition is met, the result follows.
**自然而然** (zìrán'érrán) = naturally / of its own accord — a four-character idiom meaning 'naturally and inevitably.' **说出来** = spoke out / came out: directional complement **出来** = emerge outward.

sentences.base_language: The audience began to nod along as I spoke
sentences.target_language: 听众开始随着我的讲话点头
sentences.romanized: tīngzhòng kāishǐ suízhe wǒ de jiǎnghuà diǎntóu
sentences.grammarNote:
**随着** (suízhe) = along with / as — marks something happening in accompaniment: 随着音乐 (along with the music), 随着时间 (as time goes on).
**点头** (diǎntóu) = nod: **点** (dot/tap) + **头** (head). The opposite is **摇头** (yáotóu, shake head). **听众** (tīngzhòng) = audience: **听** (listen) + **众** (crowd).

sentences.base_language: When I finished everyone applauded
sentences.target_language: 我说完的时候，大家都鼓掌了
sentences.romanized: wǒ shuō wán de shíhou, dàjiā dōu gǔzhǎng le
sentences.grammarNote:
**说完** = finished speaking — resultative complement **完** (wán) indicates the action reached completion.
**鼓掌** (gǔzhǎng) = to applaud: **鼓** (drum/beat) + **掌** (palm). **大家** (dàjiā) = everyone — more casual and warm than **所有人** (all people). 大家都 = everyone (without exception).

sentences.base_language: I was so relieved I almost cried
sentences.target_language: 我如释重负，差点哭出来
sentences.romanized: wǒ rú shì zhòng fù, chà diǎn kū chūlái
sentences.grammarNote:
**如释重负** (rú shì zhòng fù) = a 成语 (chéngyǔ) meaning 'as if a heavy burden has been lifted': **如** (like) + **释** (release) + **重负** (heavy burden). A vivid idiom for profound relief.
**差点** (chà diǎn) = almost / nearly (but didn't): 差点哭出来 = 'almost cried.' **差点** is very common for near-misses.

sentences.base_language: After that day I was no longer afraid of speaking in public
sentences.target_language: 从那天以后，我就不再害怕当众讲话了
sentences.romanized: cóng nà tiān yǐhòu, wǒ jiù bù zài hàipà dāngzhòng jiǎnghuà le
sentences.grammarNote:
**从…以后** (cóng…yǐhòu) = from… onward / after…: 从那天以后 = 'from that day on.'
**不再** (bù zài) = no longer / not anymore. **当众** (dāngzhòng) = in public / in front of everyone: **当** (facing/in the presence of) + **众** (crowd). 当众讲话 = speak in public.
